steps.title: Make the sums as close as possible
steps.content: To minimize the mean difference, use the smallest possible values for data set A and the largest possible values for data set B in each interval.

steps.title: Find the closest sums
steps.content:
Using the histogram frequencies $3,4,7,9$, the closest possible sums are
$$3(20)+4(30)+7(40)+9(50)=910$$
and
$$3(19)+4(29)+7(39)+9(49)=887$$
The smallest possible sum difference is
$$910-887=23$$

steps.title: Convert to mean difference
steps.content:
Each data set has $23$ integers, so divide the sum difference by $23$.
$$\frac{23}{23}=1$$
The answer is B.
